my_thoughts: The diagnostic works: hesitation = sleep + incomplete understanding (when one knows what is most important, one has no hesitation). The student asked diagnostic (tentative self-assessment), which shows they understand: hesitation = diagnostic. They'll likely accept the diagnostic. The episode is complete: peak optimization → reorientation → precondition → dismissal → diagnostic → diagnostic. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Three-turn pattern: T1: Student asked for peak optimization (focusing on high moments)—reorientation: regular efforts, emotional realization of sleep. T2: Student questioned precondition (seeking excuse for inaction)—dismissal: half-sleep sufficient. T3: Student asked diagnostic ('Is hesitation between two different aims a sign of sleep?')—tentative self-assessment via asks for diagnostic and relates uncertainty to state. They understand: hesitation = diagnostic (uncertainty). The none mechanism provides diagnostic: hesitation = sleep + incomplete understanding ('Partly of sleep and partly of incomplete understanding. When one knows what is most important, one has no hesitation'). Pattern complete: peak optimization → reorientation → precondition question → dismissal → diagnostic question → diagnostic. They've shifted: hesitation = sleep + incomplete understanding, not just sleep.
visible_response:
Partly of sleep and partly of incomplete understanding. When one knows what is
most important, one has no hesitation.
